TextENGNew: Sunday, October 10th, 1982.
"...Oh, Minai-san! Thank you for your service!"
"Thank you, too.@k@vS11/28/420100359. ...Long time no see, Kaji-kun. I hope you've been well?"
"O-of course! All thanks to you!"
The familiar young officer standing guard at the entrance answered in an eager voice, still holding his salute.
Having only graduated from the police academy the previous spring, he still carried a boyish innocence about him.
Smiling warmly at his earnestness, Tomoe gave him a light pat on the shoulder with a "Keep up the good work" and passed through the automatic doors.
...The moment she stepped inside, the distinctively old-fashioned air of the place tickled her nostrils.
(...Hasn't changed a bit.)
Breathing in the familiar atmosphere of her former workplace for the first time in months brought an involuntary smile to her face.
...Then again, only three months had passed since her transfer to the National Police Agency. It wasn't as though the place could have transformed overnight.
"...Whoops, can't do that."
Snapping out of her reverie, Tomoe walked over to the reception desk and addressed the female officer on duty with an "Excuse me."
Unfortunately, it was a face she didn't recognize, so She pulled out her police badge to identify herself.
"...I'm Minai from Kakiuchi Station.@k@vS11/28/420100363. I have a meeting with the section chief of the First Investigation Division, Honjou-san. Would you mind contacting him?"
The receptionist escorted her to one of the conference rooms, where she waited for a few minutes.
Before she could even touch the tea that had been set out, the door clicked open and an aging detective appeared.
"Ga ha ha, ya look just as serious as always!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2002. Ya didn't have to call me from the front window. Ya coulda come straight to the First Division office!"

"No, I didn't come here today to discuss official business.@k@vS11/28/420100365. I'll pay everyone a visit on another day and give them a proper greeting after things have calmed down.@k@vS11/28/420100366. ...Oh, I did bring you a little something."
With that, Tomoe presented the two large sake bottles she had brought to the detective now settled heavily into the chair across from her — Section Chief Honjou of the First Investigation Division.
It was a bottle of first-rate local sake from Kakiuchi.
"Whoa ho, thanks a lot~!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2004. This stuff is hard to get your hands on, so I'm thrilled to accept it!"
"I recall you mentioning once before that you wanted to drink some, Honjou-san.@k@vS11/28/420100368. I picked up two bottles just before I left."
"Then, why don't we get everyone and have a drink together?"
"No, I'm still on duty.@k@vS11/28/420100370. ...Plus, it hasn't been that long since we all celebrated at that big farewell party, so I'd be a bit embarrassed to show my face here..."
"Aah, yes, of course!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2007. You were crying at our last meeting, weren't 'cha, Tomoe-chan~?!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2007_a. Haa ha ha!"
"N-no I wasn't?!@k@vS11/28/420100372. Forget about that! It was such an embarrassing note to leave on~!!"
Remembering her farewell party, her face flushed scarlet as she waved her hands frantically.
Seeing that, Honjou burst into an even louder "Ga ha ha!!" of laughter.

"Alright~. Anyway, rumor is you're having a lot of success at your new workplace, eh?@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2009. I'd expect no less from someone chosen to be part of the elite recommended group!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2010. Our time together was short, but I'm proud of ya!"
"Stop... enough of that.@k@vS11/28/420100374. I haven't forgotten everything I've learned from you, Honjou-san, and I've been able to apply that knowledge in the field."

"Don't be so humble.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2012. I've never had a subordinate as worth teaching as you, Tomoe-chan.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2013. No matter how many times you failed and got upset, you put in the effort to reflect on every single case...@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2014. The other youngsters could learn from your example!"
"Thank you very much.@k@vS11/28/420100376. But it was all thanks to you that I was able to work so hard, Honjou-san.@k@vS11/28/420100377. That's not just flattery. I seriously mean it."
...That was no mere humility.
When Tomoe was transferred from the juvenile division to the First Investigation Division and assigned to the special investigation unit handling cold cases, Honjou had been her direct superior.
changeReason: Old 'youth department' vs new 'juvenile division'; JPN 少年課 = juvenile division. Different departmental name.

TextENGNew: Honjou was a veteran detective on the non-career track, having risen through the ranks all the way from beat cop to superintendent.
His strong Ibaraki accent, courtesy of his local roots, combined with his intensely emotional temperament meant Tomoe had clashed with him more than a few times early on.
...Yet every time she accompanied him to a crime scene or listened to him in an investigation meeting, the insight and analytical prowess born of his long experience left her stunned.
"Don't look at the criminal. Look at the crime scene."

With those words, Honjou would repeatedly drag Tomoe out of the special investigation unit's file room — where she was reviewing evidence and writing reports — and force her to visit the actual crime scenes.
...Crime scenes where the blood and bodies had long since been removed.
Where even the forensics team's markers and chalk outlines had weathered away to nothing.
Hours spent walking every inch of a place where the very traces of the crime were fading — and from there, Honjou would speak.
changeReason: Old says Honjou told Tomoe to look too; new says he would speak from the scene. Different action described.

TextENGNew: "Minai-kun.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2017. You're part of the First Division's "special investigation team" handling unresolved cases. You understand what that means, right?"
"Searching for some kind of decisive evidence to resolve the investigation quickly... right?"
"Wrong, that's not it at all."
...Honjou went on to explain.

A crime scene retains the most evidence immediately after an incident occurs... but precisely because of that abundance, investigators are prone to viewing it through the lens of preconceptions and fixed assumptions.
For example, if a bloody knife is found on the ground, they'll think it was the weapon.
The wounds and traces left on the corpse can suggest the cause of death.
As a result, a picture of the culprit can be determined from there, and the suspect can be arrested, charged, and prosecuted based on that information...
In most cases, conventional investigators are able to capture the culprit that way.
But sometimes, clinging too tightly to that initial assessment blinds investigators to hidden secrets or tricks — leaving the culprit beyond their reach.
When such unresolved cases sit dormant past a certain period, the investigation headquarters is disbanded and the case is handed off to the special investigation unit as a 'continuation.'
changeReason: Old 'abandoned by headquarters' vs new 'investigation headquarters is disbanded'; JPN 捜査本部が解散 = HQ disbanded, a formal procedural distinction.

TextENGNew: It then falls to the special investigators to reexamine all existing materials and determine whether anything was overlooked.
"That said, you don't think the process of handling unresolved cases is more about settling unfinished business, do ya?"
"W-well..."
She couldn't deny what he'd said. He'd hit her right where it hurt.
By definition, an unresolved case was one that veteran detectives and forensic specialists had pored over desperately, yet still failed to crack.
What chance did a greenhorn like her — barely understanding the basics of investigation — have of finding a breakthrough by flipping through old files?
...That was what Tomoe had believed, deep down. And so it was true that she had treated the work of investigating cold cases as little more than organizing paperwork.

"...The First Investigation Division doesn't need a simple investigation material reorganizer.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2021. The special investigator is what Western criminal psychology analysts refer to as a profiler."
"Profiler... huh?"
"That's why I bring ya to the crime scenes."
Honjou pointed at the crime scene — now devoid of any atmosphere suggesting a crime had ever occurred — and spoke.
changeReason: Old 'Honjou said you can't feel the atmosphere' vs new 'Honjou pointed at the scene and spoke'; JPN describes pointing at a scene where atmosphere is gone.

TextENGNew: A crime scene will always retain some indelible trace of the perpetrator. And if the crime was murder, all the more so.
For example, the criminal's footprints. ...Even if they don't remain in so simple a form.
Where did the culprit go before the crime?
And how did he get away after the crime?
The method.
The route.
By examining these items one by one, you can identify what psychological state the culprit was in, and are naturally able to see what he intended to do.

"Profiling investigation techniques aren't very well known in Japan, but they're already being practiced in the West, and getting results.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2024. I see it becoming a mainstay of investigations in the future."
"Honjou-san..."
"Crimes have gotten more and more diverse, and their techniques more sophisticated.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2026. So, it's only natural for police to adopt these new methods."

"Our role is to catch the culprit.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2028. In order to do that, we should use any weapon at our disposal.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2029. Knowledge and experience are what's needed to survive as a detective. Not being a stickler for authority."
"...Right."
Older people tend to be more hard-headed, but Honjou had spent time working on the front lines, so he of all people understood the urgency and impending danger.
Tomoe genuinely wanted to learn from his eagerness and flexible insights.
Additionally, Honjou taught her to pay careful attention to the people related to the victim after the incident.
Has anyone gained some benefit from the victim's death?
Is anyone exhibiting suspicious movements after the incident?
In general, these behavioral patterns can't be seen immediately after the incident. ...Those ashamed of their dark secrets are aware that any misbehavior would give the police reason to suspect them.
However, once police activity seems to slow down... or when they reach the limit of their patience... that's when these behaviors manifest.
And since many of those actions can cast suspicion on them, you can often find clues with a little careful attention.
Thus, by finding new ideas and clues as time moves on, you can solve incidents that on-site investigators had no choice but to abandon. ...That was the job of the special investigator.
"...Even though it's been years since you taught me that, I still remember it like it was yesterday."
"Tahaha... I did say something like that."
Tomoe's eyes narrowed as she reminisced about old times, and she gazed at Honjou as he attempted to hide his embarrassment by gulping down his coffee.
...Back then, she was a psychology major who had gained insight during her time working in the youth division.
No... looking back now, I can say that confidence was closer to complacency.
changeReason: Old says 'motivated by pride'; new says 'closer to complacency' matching 慢心 (complacency/overconfidence).

TextENGNew: So at first glance, a special investigation officer seemed to her like someone who managed materials on unresolved cases, rather than a person actually handling real cases.
But once she spent times on the front lines... she recognized it more as being conscious of knowledge and information.
Honjou could tell from Tomoe's regular behavior that she was sharp. He spoke to her with a serious expression on his face to cut off any pride from the root.
"There's no such thing as a perfect crime.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2032. If you carefully examine the clues, you can always find the answer."

"...So, the detective acting as the special investigator needs to be a talented individual with deep analytical skills and a broad perspective.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2034. Not some clerk gathering documents."

For someone as focused on improvement as Tomoe, a harsh reprimand like that was like being splashed with cold water... But simultaneously, it was a pivotal moment to reflect on her own pride and inability.
Without those experiences as a special investigator, she would've become an uninspired detective, unable to challenge methods of investigation.
After that, she memorized and analyzed an unthinkable amount of information related to past cases.

As a result, Tomoe was able to achieve exceptional results. If not for that, she would never have been selected for the recommended group, and probably would've gone on working at some prefectural police station somewhere...
(So, it wasn't my latent ability that got me chosen for the recommended group.@k@vS11/28/420100385. It was the perspective and techniques I learned while working under Honjou-san...)
As she pondered that, Tomoe once again realized how lucky she was.

Come to think of it, that was also true of the youth department. Back then, Section Chief Kishimoto had already retired to the countryside, but he taught her how to connect with kids with various problems.
He also thought about Tomoe's excessively straight-laced conduct and way of thinking... and gave her earnest advice on how to rectify it.
In that sense, she was blessed with supportive superiors even as far back as her time with the Ibaraki prefectural police department.
"That's why I'll keep on doing my best, without forgetting what brought me here.@k@vS11/28/420100387. Please, accept my thanks for your continued guidance, both now and in the future."
"Ga ha ha!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2036. I get it. Leave it to me!@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2037. Next time you come back, I'll try to be useful~!"
"Ahahahaha!"
Tomoe's business here should've just been a friendly greeting, but thanks to the incident she found out about yesterday she had "serious" business to attend to.
That was the reason she requested they talk in the meeting room, rather than the open reception area.
"...So, about what we discussed on the phone."
"Aah, right.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2039. ...Here's the document."
Honjou spread a document open on the opposite side of the table, with a serious expression completely lacking the earlier jovial atmosphere.
There was a detailed report from the scene of the incident, and a photo of the scene was clipped to the side.
"...An awful situation."

"He fell from the window on the eighth floor, so his face was trashed beyond recognition.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2041. His parent confirmed the identity based on the pajamas he always wore and a mole on his shoulder.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2042. ...But I couldn't show them the face."
She glanced at the picture, then quickly flipped it over. ...It was painfully difficult to bear.
(Absolutely... I could never show this to his parents.)
She shook her head to chase off her harrowing thoughts, and drew a deep breath to calm herself.
She didn't even feel like confirming the "mole" that Honjou pointed out.
Even though she had very little conversation with the victim, Kouhei Sawamura, she did recognize the face from an old case she worked on with Ooishi.
And that made the difference between her memories of him in life and his current state... that much more painful.

"The victim was already dead at the time he was discovered.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2044. Most likely, instant death.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2045. We haven't found anything resembling a will in the victim's hospital room, but...@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2046. We're investigating the possibilities of both suicide and an accident."
"...@k@vS11/28/420100394.And what is the possibility of murder?"

"It was a closed room. The hospital room was locked at the time of the incident.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2048. Even after listening to the testimony of several nurses, nobody went in and out of the room after the last time they took his temperature."

"And the footage recorded by the security cameras didn't reveal any suspicious personnel...@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2050. So, either he jumped down of his own volition, or he was trying to peek out the window and lost his footing.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2051. ...The official opinion is one or the other."
"...Is that so?"
Tomoe also thought that reasoning sounded valid.
There was no doubt that Kouhei Sawamura was deeply mentally scarred after the earlier incident. Tomoe could assert to that herself.
Therefore, even though there was a possibility the fall was an accident... it was far more likely that his mental instability drove him to jump.
Plus, the testimony of the nurses and the fact that the tapes didn't reveal anyone suspicious were immutable evidence.
However...
(...Something doesn't add up.)
In fact, she was starting to feel suspicious because the conditions were a little too consistent.
Plus, thinking about it from the victim's point of view, there were clearly a few odd points...
"...What is it, Tomoe-chan?"
"The day the victim fell was a Saturday, right?@k@vS11/28/420100398. Why that day in particular?"
"? ...What are you getting at?"

"Something I learned while working a different case a little while ago.@k@vS11/28/420100400. ...Every Saturday, the victim's female friend... or rather, his girlfriend, would travel from a long distance to visit him."
She pulled out her police notebook and flipped through it.
...Tomoe could still remember the contents since it was so recent, so she was able to find the page easily.

"...Just as I thought.@k@vS11/28/420100402. And not only that... Look here, the first person to discover the body was a female student named "Nagisa Ozaki."@k@vS11/28/420100403. And she was dating the victim at his old school."
"Hmm... so?"
"Maybe I'm only saying this because I'm a woman.@k@vS11/28/420100405. ...But do you think he'd jump to his death without meeting her on the day she always comes to visit?"
"That doesn't sit right with me."
The thoughts that drive a person to suicide and an escape from reality are often accompanied by a desire to share those final thoughts and intentions with someone other than yourself.
For example, resentment.
Apology.
Despair.
Thus, the method and location of death can in many cases reveal something about the victim's intentions.
Among the methods of suicide, psychological studies consider jumping a very egocentric method.
First of all, you can't even imagine how much your body will be destroyed by the impact.
...And there is a strange sense of elation and freedom that comes from showing that death to others.
In other words, the emotional motivations include intense despair and twisted exhibitionism.
(Jumping suicide is an act intended to garner sympathy by showing your ugly form to the people around you, or to force guilt upon the person who has wronged you.)
(If so, could he have truly felt such dark feelings while waiting for his girlfriend to come visit him...?)
Tomoe simply couldn't convince herself of that.
"...Hoh, you've really been practicing properly.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2056. Now that's behavioral analysis."
Honjou had a huge grin on his face, since he was thinking the same thing. Tomoe couldn't help but respond with a wry smile.
In any event, this was her first time seeing the case files, so she came up with that thought immediately. Battle-hardened Honjou realized that right away...
"So, Honjou-san... does that mean you have the same opinion?"

"The forensics and precinct staff see it as a seven in 10 chance of being suicide.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2058. I, then again, read it as a seven in 10 chance of murder.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_honjo2059. I was planning to take it up with the First Division... but now I'm much more interested in hearing your thoughts, Tomoe-chan."
"And what's the basis for your assessment?"
"...This."
Honjou pulled two photos out of his breast pocket as he said that, then handed them to Tomoe.

The first picture was an enlarged photo of a window frame, taken at night. ...Scratch marks were left on the bottom and right side of the window frame, and the faint light of a luminol reaction could be seen to the right.
And the second picture... showed the back of a left hand laying half-opened on the asphalt, most likely belonging to the victim.
The nail on the index finger... was covered in blood and peeling off.
"...That's right. I was in charge of Sawamura-san.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2088. Here are his records."
The older nurse named Hiranuma pulled a file out of the bookshelf as she said that, then handed it to Tomoe.
She seemed to have a meticulous personality, and gave a polite but thorough description.

"There was a malfunction in the fire alarm after we measured his temperature, so I had to visit every room to deal with it before returning to this station.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2090. After that, a friend of his came to visit around 4 o'clock, so I escorted her to his room."
"I heard from the other detectives that his room was locked?"
"Right. I thought it was strange, because he normally didn't lock his room."
Tomoe quickly skimmed through the file's contents while flipping through the pages.
...It was almost identical to the report summarized in the investigation materials.
"...Can you show me the patient's room?"
"Yes, of course.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2093. Right this way."
"...So, he fell from here?"
"Right. His friend was the first to discover him, but she started to panic.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2095. ...I had a hard time restraining her."
She was most likely confused and unable to accept this as reality. It was far too burdensome a tale for an early adolescent.

"So, you reported it to the police immediately after discovery.@k@vS11/28/420100416. Then, you contacted the doctor in charge at the center station.@k@vS11/28/420100417. ...I'm not mistaken about any of that, right?"
"Yes, that is correct."
"I see."
Tomoe nodded while scribbling Hiranuma's story into her notebook. Then, she turned her attention toward the window frame in the photo.
"...These scratches were caused by the victim.@k@vS11/28/420100420. But why are they here?"
"Unfortunately, I have no idea...@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2098. Perhaps he lost his balance while looking outside, and tried to grab the window frame."
"...@k@vS11/28/420100422.At any rate, why aren't there any bars on the window of this hospital room?"
Tomoe directly asked about the matter that was bothering her.
The victim was being hospitalized on the top floor, so it wouldn't be surprising for this hospital to have them, in consideration for the patient's safety.
Not to mention, the patients hospitalized in this particular ward were...
"Let me ask another question then.@k@vS11/28/420100424. ...All the patients hospitalized on this floor have been diagnosed for psychiatric treatment, correct?"
"Yes, that's right.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2100. That's why the doors are designed so they can be locked from the outside."
"In which case, why have no measures been taken to prevent falling out of the windows?"

"Iron bars often cause a patient to feel confined, which has an adverse effect on their treatment.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2102. ...Plus, the windows are equipped with locks, so they can't simply be opened."
"And yet, this one was open.@k@vS11/28/420100427. ...Why is that?"
"...I'm sorry, that was an oversight on our part.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2104. I unlocked it once to air the room out, but forgot about it.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2105. ...It's inexcusable."
Next, Tomoe examined the calendar on the wall beside the bed.
Cutesy colorful characters were printed on it. It looked like it was designed for girls.
"...Did this belong to the victim?"
"No, his friend Ozaki-san brought that."
"I see..."
She carefully pinched the page to see the next page underneath. It was currently October, so there were two more pages.
And finally, Tomoe approached the door, pushed the button on the knob, and closed it.
It wasn't a normal door where you turn a tab to lock the door. The lock apparently prevented the outer knob from rotating.
So, rather than locking before closing, the mechanism required the door to be closed before locking.
"By the way, you mentioned that the fire alarm went off some time before the victim was discovered.@k@vS11/28/420100433. Do you know approximately what time that happened?"
"Umm... I think it was around 3 o'clock.@k@vS11/00/VT3B_hiranu2108. I went and looked around for a while when it went off, so it probably continued for about three minutes."
"...Understood. Thank you very much."
Tomoe gave her a bow, then made her way out of the hospital.
The autumn sky was clear and a refreshing breeze blew by.
(Rather warm today.)
Feeling a little sweat on her forehead, She pulled the handkerchief from her pocket and wiped it away.
The cobblestone-paved path glittered in the sunlight, scattering grains of light all about.
"Seems like the cicadas have already stopped crying.@k@vS11/28/420100437. ...Oh."
The autumn leaves fell down and fluttered right before her eyes.
The base of a distant mountain showed the mix of vermillion and yellow hues, and the leaves of the surrounding trees were just as vividly colored.
(How pretty.@k@vS11/28/420100439. ...If not for the current location.)
Tomoe let out a sigh, then looked over the surrounding area.
She was currently a short distance west of the city center... in a dilapidated cemetery.
Using the map given to her by the groundskeeper as a guide, she noticed another person near her destination. Moving closer to get a better look... it was a girl she recognized, Nagisa Ozaki.
Before long, Nagisa noticed the approaching footsteps and turned toward Tomoe. Then she lightly bowed her head before turning her gaze back toward the gravestone in front of her.
"Long time no see.@k@vS11/28/420100442. ...Would it be all right for me to pay him a visit?"
Nagisa gave no answer to that... and simply shuffled her feet so she wasn't standing directly in front of the gravestone.
Tomoe took that as affirmation and said, "Thank you," then sat the chrysanthemums she bought from the local flower shop down next to the grave.
There was already a beautifully blooming cosmos flower sitting there. ...Most likely placed there by Nagisa herself.
She lit a bundle of incense sticks at the nearby brazier, then sat them down in the indentations of the incense burner.
"I didn't want to see him again like this..."
"...@k@vS11/28/420100446.It might be an odd thing for me to say, but I wanted him to get better quickly and go back to the way he was before.@k@vS11/28/420100447. Both him... and you, too."
Tomoe could hear a heavy breath, even though her back was turned.
...Honestly, there were several things she wanted to ask Nagisa about regarding this case.
However... Tomoe recognized how much the girl's heart must ache, so she repressed the urge.
The person most hurt by this incident... was none other than Nagisa. Tomoe wasn't so callous as to force that on her just because of the opportunity.
"I'm sorry for intruding.@k@vS11/28/420100449. ...Anyway, take care."
After saying only that, She turned away from Nagisa...
"...He was only just laid to rest here."
That moment.
She looked back as soon as she heard Nagisa muttering.
Nagisa clenched her blouse near her chest and furrowed her eyebrows to endure the emotions welling up inside her.

"When I got down to the first floor, he was already covered in a white cloth and hospital staff wouldn't let me get close.@k@vS11/35/420500091. The police carried him away immediately after that, and by the time I saw him again he was already ashes."
"In the end... I never got to see Kouhei-kun's face."
Tomoe thought that outcome was for the best. Nothing good would come of showing such a drastically warped human form to a child.
In fact, Madoka was so scarred by seeing her parents' burned bodies that she was still suffering from it even now...

"Maybe that's why...@k@vS11/35/420500094. I still can't believe it.@k@vS11/35/420500095. Is Kouhei-kun really dead?@k@vS11/35/420500096. It's not just a hoax that everyone will tease me about later...?"
"...Ozaki-san."

"Kouhei-kun escaped from his hospital room right after he was admitted there.@k@vS11/35/420500098. Since the door was locked, he broke the window, still dressed in his pajamas...@k@vS11/35/420500099. He had a terrible dream, so he was so scared."
...It had probably happened a little while after Tomoe had gone in to question him.
At the time, she was heading to the hospital room in the back of the first floor to meet him, but his parents refused to cooperate, so she was about to leave... and that was when she met Nagisa.
And when the parents weren't there, she was able to meet him face to face once with Nagisa's consent.
Of course, she was only able to make small talk, but his pale worn-out appearance was plain to see during their short time together...

"But he was so happy when his hospital room was moved from the first floor to the eighth, the week before last.@k@vS11/35/420500101. He said the view around there was really nice, and the nurse told him that patients were moved to that floor when their condition was improving."

"Plus, last time I saw him he said he was going to learn the date of his discharge after his diagnosis the following week.@k@vS11/35/420500103. So when I heard that, I decided to bring him the most beautiful flowers I could find on that day..."

"He promised that he'd get out of the hospital soon and we'd go to college together...@k@vS11/35/420500105. Kouhei-kun was really smart, so I was working really hard to get into the place where he wanted to go.@k@vS11/35/420500106. And yet...!"
Nagisa's voice trembled... while huge tears spilled from her eyes.
The drops came overflowing down her cheeks, one after another... and no matter how much she wiped them away with the back of her hands, they wouldn't stop.
"...Detective."
While still sobbing, she looked up and turned toward Tomoe. And then,.
"So that's it after all.@k@vS11/35/420500109. ...It's the "curse of Oyashiro-sama" that Reina mentioned?"
"Because it's so strange, isn't it?!"
"Just like everyone said, Kouhei-kun did occasionally have weird dreams and nightmares.@k@vS11/35/420500112. But... I can't believe he would ignore his promise to me and want to kill himself...!"
Seeing the desperately sorrowful color of her eyes... painfully squeezed at Tomoe's chest.
"This is all because of Reina...!@k@vS11/35/420500114. But she just disappeared without apologizing!@k@vS11/35/420500115. All because of that girl. That girl...!"
"...@k@vS11/28/420100456.But Ryuugu-san was the reason you were even introduced to Sawamura-kun.@k@vS11/28/420100457. And yet... how could such a kind-hearted girl have even considered hurting him?"
"I don't know!@k@vS11/35/420500117. How could I...?!"
"Calm down, Ozaki-san.@k@vS11/28/420100459. ...If we're going to catch the person who killed Kouhei-kun, we'll have to think clearly."
Tomoe lowered her face to Nagisa's height to look her in the eyes, and gave her a gentle look to calm her down.
Looking at that expression, Nagisa relaxed a little of the anger and frustration welling up inside her... She took a moment to idly think, then muttered quietly.
"...Detective."
"What's it?"
"Do you think... that Kouhei-kun was murdered?"
"That's right, I can't imagine it's anything else.@k@vS11/28/420100462. I think someone killed him."
After hearing that... Nagisa began spilling huge tears once again.
However, they had a slightly different tone than the angry, sorrowful tears from a moment ago.
"...Nobody would believe me.@k@vS11/35/420500123. Kouhei-kun wouldn't take his own life.@k@vS11/35/420500124. And an accident is even less likely..."

"Nevertheless, everyone arbitrarily decided it was an accident or suicide, just because it was a closed room...!@k@vS11/35/420500126. Even though I begged them to look for the killer, nobody would...!"
"...It's all right.@k@vS11/28/420100464. Because at the very least, I agree with you."
"Seriously...?"
"That's right.@k@vS11/28/420100466. You said just now, a week before his death Sawamura-kun said "he might be able to leave in a week, depending on his diagnosis," right?"
Nagisa deeply nodded. ...What Nagisa had said right there completely stripped away the uneasy sensation Tomoe was feeling.

"It's just as you said. When I checked the calendar in his room, his scheduled discharge date was written on the next month.@k@vS11/28/420100468. I think he was planning to tell you about it when you got here."
"...And yet, he'd commit suicide on the day of your arrival without telling you? Impossible.@k@vS11/28/420100470. No matter how you think about it, it's unnatural."
"Th-then...?"

"And of course, there's no chance it was an accident.@k@vS11/28/420100472. There's no reason for him to get close enough to the window to fall to begin with, and the last nurse that spoke with him said that nothing was unusual when his temperature was taken.@k@vS11/28/420100473. ...This is a murder, no doubt about it."
Then, Tomoe stood up straight and glanced at her watch. ...She still had a little more time before the express train arrived.
"Ozaki-san.@k@vS11/28/420100475. This may be painful for you, but I'd like your cooperation in order to find the truth.@k@vS11/28/420100476. ...Do you have a little time after this?"
"I promise you.@k@vS11/28/420100478. No matter what, I'll catch the killer.@k@vS11/28/420100479. ...All right?"
"...Okay."
